misconceptionSummary: Students order decimals by digit count rather than by column value.

lines.text: All now have three decimal places

steps.narration: Before ordering, pad every decimal with trailing zeros until they all share the same number of decimal places. The values are unchanged — you are just making the columns line up.
Compare the tenths column first. Three tenths is least, then four tenths, then six tenths. The tenths column settles the order without needing to look any further right.
Strip the padding zeros and write the original forms in order. Ascending goes smallest to largest; descending goes largest to smallest.

visuals.label: Pad → compare tenths → order smallest to largest

steps.narration: Pad, compare column by column, then write in order. Three steps every time you need to sort a list of decimals.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: ordering by digit count and placing 0.6 first because it is 'short'. Pad to the same length first — 0.600 is the largest here.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: Digit count tells you nothing about size. Pad everything to equal length, then compare the tenths column first.
